title: manulea longiflora
intro: Manulea longiflora, also known as the red salvia or crimson sage, is a plant species that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and treat wounds. The health benefits of Manulea longiflora include its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Manulea longiflora can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in herbal remedies for its medicinal properties.
